t_ch: 哈哈，你这话说得就很有姬子读书时的风范。确实是我的错，真抱歉。
t_en: Haha, that sounds just like something Himeko would've said back in her school days. You're right, it was my fault. I'm really sorry.

t_ch: 还是不谈这些了。你这次来找我，是有什么事吗？
t_en: But let's not dwell on that. So, what brings you here today?

t_ch: 又见面了，姬子的伙伴。今天找我有什么事吗？
t_en: It's good to see you again, Himeko's companion. Is there something you need from me today?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊天。
t_en: I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 当然可以。你想聊什么话题呢？
t_en: Certainly. What would you like to discuss?

t_ch: 姬子为什么这么信任你？
t_en: Why is it that Himeko trusts you so much?

t_ch: 我们算是忘年交了。在这所学校里，就属我和她关系最好了。
t_en: Our friendship transcends age. Out of everyone here at the school, she and I were the closest.

t_ch: 姬子在这读书的时候，性格可是相当爆炸。别说学生们了，就连老师有什么过分的言行，她都敢站出来顶撞。
t_en: Back in her student days, Himeko had quite a fiery personality. She wouldn't just stand up to students. Even teachers got called out if they went too far.

t_ch: 但也不知为什么，她对幻造和物理学的知识感兴趣——我以前在飞登行所之类的航天企业里干过一阵子。她总是问我星际航行相关的话题。
t_en: But for some reason, she was interested in imagenesis and physics. I used to work for a while at aerospace companies like AscendAir, and she was always asking me about interstellar travel.

t_ch: 时间一久，连生活里遇到的烦恼和快乐也会和我分享。你知道的，我行动不方便，只能乖乖听着她滔滔不绝地讲。
t_en: Over time, she started sharing the ups and downs of her life with me too. You know how it is. I couldn't exactly move around, so I just sat there and listened to her ramble on and on.

t_ch: 这大概就是她信任我的原因吧。
t_en: I guess that's probably why she trusts me.

t_ch: 「幻造课」究竟是学什么的？
t_en: What exactly do you learn in the Imagenesis class?

t_ch: 用最简单的话来讲，就是「使用愿力的技术」。
t_en: In the simplest terms, it's the craft of harnessing Wishpower.

t_ch: 在这个世界，不止是创造幻造种，很多事都需要使用愿力来达成。建筑、工业、医疗、农耕…「幻造」可以说是一切领域的基本原理。
t_en: In this world, it takes Wishpower to do many things, not just create imagenae. Architecture, industry, medicine, agriculture, you name it... And imagenesis is the fundamental principle behind it all.

t_ch: 愿力的使用绝不是只要有画笔和想像就能付诸现实的，要让幻造之物呈现符合经验认知与规律的结构，学生仍然少不了对自然科学的学习。
t_en: Using Wishpower isn't just about having a brush and imagination to make things real. If students want their imagenated creations to actually have structure that makes sense and follows the rules of reality, they've still gotta hit the books on natural sciences.

t_ch: 可以分享一些姬子的糗事吗？
t_en: Got any embarrassing stories about Himeko?

t_ch: 和别人大谈自己学生的糗事…有违老师的职业道德吧！
t_en: Gossiping about your students' embarrassing moments to other people... That's gotta violate the teacher's code of ethics, I'd say!

t_ch: 不过嘛…电视剧里不是常有这样的桥段吗？学生一不小心喊老师「妈妈」，引来了全班同学的哄堂大笑。
t_en: But then again... you know how there's always that trope in TV shows? The one where a student accidentally calls their teacher "Mom," and the whole class bursts out laughing?

t_ch: 这件事姬子也干过哦～只不过，毕竟是姬子，同学们全都噤若寒蝉，憋紫了脸也不敢笑——只有我一个人在大声嘲笑，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Well, that happened with Himeko, too~ Except, since it was Himeko, the whole class was dead silent. They were trying so hard not to laugh that their faces were turning purple. I was the only one laughing out loud, hahaha!

t_ch: 后来，我被迫做了一个月她的拳击陪练，她才原谅了我。
t_en: After that, I had to be her punching bag for a whole month before she'd forgive me.

t_ch: 咳咳，这个话题还是到此为止吧！
t_en: Ahem... let's just leave it at that!

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about.

t_ch: 有空的话，欢迎你随时来找我。
t_en: Feel free to swing by anytime you're free.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about.

t_ch: 有空的话，欢迎你随时来找我。
t_en: Feel free to swing by anytime you're free.

t_ch: 哔…哔哔哔_/\___/\____哔波！难怪本狸突然接收到了一串令人心潮起伏的信号，原来是浣熊大人来杂志社视察工作啵！
t_en: Zip... Zzz-zzap_/\___/\____zap! No wonder I suddenly picked up such a heart-pounding signal. It's Great Raccoon! Here to inspect our work, zip!

t_ch: 本狸现在一心只想和浣熊大人一起复兴杂志社，成为最可靠的狸记者！
t_en: My only mission is to revive the publishing house with Great Raccoon and become the most reliable furbo reporter ever!

t_ch: 听说你的本名…不叫幻太子？
t_en: So your real name... isn't Paraprince?

t_ch: 哔波，这是什么话，人家当然就叫幻太子。
t_en: Zap, what are you talking about? Of course my name is Paraprince.

t_ch: 浣熊大人一定听过百变狸猫的故事啵！狸猫一族是二相乐园的古老幻造种，能变成天上飞的疾锋龙，水里游的噗噜。
t_en: Surely the Great Raccoon has heard the stories of the Shapeshifting Furbo, right!? The furbo clan is an ancient line of imagenae in Planarcadia. We can transform into Bullet Wyrms that soar through the sky or ploos that swim in the waters.

t_ch: 只要想变，变成青春可爱的美少女什么的也不在话下。
t_en: We can even transform into an adorable young maiden if we so desire.

t_ch: 但你明明还是一只狸猫？
t_en: But you're obviously still a furbo right now?

t_ch: 浣熊大人看清楚啵，人家现在是<color=#dbc291ff>狸猫当中的美少女</color>！如果是要看人类眼中的美少女的话…有点可惜，浣熊大人现在积攒的愿力还不够啵！
t_en: Look closer, Great Raccoon. Right now, I'm a <color=#dbc291ff>maiden among furbos</color>! If you wanted to see a maiden by human standards... that's too bad. You don't have enough Wishpower for that yet!

t_ch: 我没说我要看…
t_en: I never said I wanted to see that...

t_ch: 哔…嘟……难道说，浣熊大人是主张狸猫不能随意变身的守旧派？正好正好，像我这样乖乖做狸猫的狸子，不多见啵！
t_en: Zip... zap... Don't tell me the Great Raccoon is one of those traditionalists who think furbos shouldn't just transform whenever they want? Perfect! A well-behaved furbo like me is a rare find!

t_ch: 为什么是要我的愿力？
t_en: Why do you need my Wishpower for that?

t_ch: 美少女的诞生来自于许下「想看美少女」的愿望的人，这很河狸。
t_en: A beautiful maiden is born from the wish of a person who wants to see one. That makes perfect sense.

t_ch: 「哔波」是什么意思？
t_en: And what does "zip-zap" mean?

t_ch: 哔波，被发现了！既然如此，就和浣熊大人说一说我的科学考察结果——
t_en: Zap! You noticed that, huh? Very well, I'll tell you about the results of my scientific investigation, O Great Raccoon...

t_ch: 经过我的研究，电视塔上的那只雷虬，一直在向二相乐园人发射不明电磁波！所以，本狸总是能听到一些神奇的电波讯号。
t_en: My research indicates that the thundercoil atop the TV tower is constantly emitting some unknown electromagnetic waves at the people of Planarcadia! That's why I'm always picking up these mysterious signals.

t_ch: 最神奇的是，整个二相乐园只有我能听得懂！虽然其他狸猫听不出来，但我接收到的每一句「哔波」，意思都不一样啵！
t_en: The most incredible part is, I'm the only one in all of Planarcadia who can understand them! The other furbo can't tell, but every "zip-zap" I receive has a unique meaning!

t_ch: 懂了，你就是传说中的电报员？
t_en: I see. So you're what they call a "telegraph operator"?

t_ch: 哔波，美少女狸猫幻太子电报员，参见啵！
t_en: Zip-zap! Beautiful maiden Furbo Paraprince telegraphist, reporting for duty!

t_ch: 哔…哔波！
t_en: Zip... zap!

t_ch: 哔波！太好了，浣熊大人和我对上电波了啵！
t_en: Zip! Fantastic! The Great Raccoon and I are on the same wavelength!

t_ch: 那你之前为什么躲着我？
t_en: Then why were you avoiding me earlier?

t_ch: 第一次见面，我怕生啵。当然，更怕熟——还请不要把咱做成狸猫火锅，我会好好干活的，拜托了！
t_en: I'm shy when meeting people for the first time. But getting to know them is even scarier! Please don't turn me into furbo hot pot! I'll work really hard, I swear!

t_ch: 哔波，浣熊大人，我会为了杂志社复兴好好干活的。
t_en: Zap! Great Raccoon, I will work my hardest for our publishing house's revival.

t_ch: 不管是从前，还是现在，《狸狸周刊》最可靠的狸猫记者必然都是我幻太子啵。
t_en: From the beginning until now, the most dependable furbo reporter at the Furbobo Weekly has always been me, Paraprince!

t_ch: 想听听杂志社从前的故事…
t_en: Tell me some stories about the publishing house's past...

t_ch: 知无不言，言无不尽，浣熊大人想知道什么，我一定全都告诉你。
t_en: I'll tell you anything you want to know. There are no secrets between us. Whatever Great Raccoon wants to know, I won't leave out any details.

t_ch: 上一位社长…
t_en: Tell me about the last director...

t_ch: 哔波哔哔哔哔——特级警报触发！虚照大人特意关照过咱们，不要随便透露她的信息啵……
t_en: Zip-zip-zap-zap — Class-A alert triggered! Lady Nihilux gave us specific instructions not to reveal any information about her... zip!

t_ch: 你这不是说了她的名字吗？
t_en: But you just said her name, you know.

t_ch: 普狸策早就和我说了。
t_en: Furbulitzer already told me about her.

t_ch: 居然被普狸策抢了先，那…那我就斗胆和浣熊大人说一说。
t_en: Furbulitzer already spilled the beans? Th—Then I guess I can risk telling you a little bit, Great Raccoon.

t_ch: 说到虚照大人，本狸脑海中浮现出第一个词是：危险危险危险——
t_en: When it comes to Lady Nihilux, the first words that pop into my head are: Danger, danger, danger...

t_ch: 虚照大人最喜欢把「把你煮成狸猫火锅哦！」挂在嘴边，她发来的消息是一定要秒回的，但发给虚照大人的消息她是看心情回的。
t_en: Lady Nihilux's favorite catchphrase is, "I'll cook you into a furbo hot pot!" You have to reply to her messages the second you get them, but she only replies to yours when she feels like it.

t_ch: 大约很久很久很久以前，虚照大人还是发过薪水的…应该发过吧？记不清啵！不过大人在的时候…我们闲散啵！直到她回来的次数越来越少……
t_en: A long, long, long time ago, Lady Nihilux actually paid us... I think? It's hard to remember! But when she was here... we took it easy! Then she started coming around less and less often...

t_ch: 杂志社也越来越周转不开，我们斗胆汇报，只得到最后一条消息：我正在无限期休假中，接下来你们要骗、要偷袭都行，让星穹列车来的浣熊接手《狸狸周刊》吧^^
t_en: The publishing house's finances got worse and worse, so we mustered the courage to report it. All we got back was this final message: "I'm on indefinite vacation. I don't care if you have to lie, cheat, or ambush someone, just get the raccoon from the Astral Express to take over Furbobo Weekly. ^^"

t_ch: 于是…于是…就是浣熊大人看到的这样啵！
t_en: And so... and so... that's how things ended up this way!

t_ch: 原来我被做局了。
t_en: So it was a setup.

t_ch: 哔哔，不是做局，浣熊大人是来做好事的啵！
t_en: Zap-zap, it's not a setup! The Great Raccoon is here to do something good!

t_ch: 那她到底去哪里了？
t_en: So where did she go, then?

t_ch: 哔波！定位失败。但以我的直觉，虚照大人还会经常在某个角落看着我们啵！
t_en: Zip! Location tracking failed. But I have a gut feeling that Lady Nihilux is still watching us from somewhere!

t_ch: 杂志社的经营状况…
t_en: The publishing house's financial situation...

t_ch: ——稳中向好，一定会稳中向好的！
t_en: It's stable and looking up! Definitely improving!

t_ch: 虚照大人说过：生而为人，即使身处险境，也要面带微笑、做好从容化为一锅鲜美至极的火锅的准备。抱着这样的气魄，浣熊大人必定会带着咱们复兴《狸狸周刊》啵。
t_en: Lady Nihilux once said: "Even when facing mortal danger, one must smile and be prepared to become a supremely delicious hot pot. With that kind of courage, Great Raccoon is sure to lead us to revive the Furbobo Weekly."

t_ch: 虚照大人真这么说？
t_en: Did Lady Nihilux actually say that?

t_ch: 是我结合狸猫们的情况，添油加醋了一下啵！
t_en: I took the liberty of spicing up the story based on the other furbos!

t_ch: 关于其他狸猫们…
t_en: Tell me more about the other furbos...

t_ch: 浣熊大人已经见过普狸策了，偷拍不好，但普狸策是为了杂志社好，笨手笨脚了点，浣熊大人别怪它啵。
t_en: Oh, you've met Furbulitzer, Great Raccoon! I know taking candid photos is bad, but he really did it for the sake of the publishing house. He can be a bit clumsy, so please try not to be too hard on him!

t_ch: 从前《狸狸周刊》有不少狸猫，但杂志社生意萧条以后，它们逃的逃，走的走，还有的卷走了大家压箱底的私房储蓄……
t_en: A lot of furbos used to work at the Furbobo Weekly. But after the publishing house fell on hard times... Some fled, others just left, and a few even made off with everyone's rainy-day funds...

t_ch: 我不怪它们，狸猫也有养家糊口的事要做。也许有一天，杂志社好起来了，它们也会回心转意，回来一起工作的。
t_en: I can't blame them, though. Those furbos have families to feed. Maybe someday, when the publishing house gets back on its feet, they'll have a change of heart and come back.

t_ch: 哔哔，当然，最值得托付重任的还是我幻太子！如果浣熊大人遇到了困难，就请传位于我…不，我的意思是我一定能帮浣熊大人分担啵。
t_en: Beep! Of course, the most dependable one of all is me, Paraprince! Should you ever find yourself in a pinch, Great Raccoon, feel free to hand over the reins to me... Ahem, I mean, I can definitely help you share the load!

t_ch: 没什么要问的了…
t_en: That's all I wanted to know...

t_ch: 怎么才能听到哔波的信号？
t_en: How can I hear the zip-zap signal?

t_ch: 哔波！说到这个我就来劲了。
t_en: Zip-zap! Now you're talking! This is my favorite subject.

t_ch: 方法很简单，如果单凭两只耳朵听不到，在头上戴一个铝制的锅，就能接听到来自雷虬塔的神奇信号。
t_en: It's quite simple. If you can't hear it with your ears alone, just put an aluminum pot on your head. You'll be able to pick up the mystical signals from Thundercoil Tower.

t_ch: 从前我建议过一个噗噜这么做，但被治安官发现，勒令它摘下了啵！理由是破坏鸽川区的区容区貌。
t_en: I told a ploo to try it once, but an officer found it and made it take it off! The reason given was that it was "detrimental to the public image of Dovebrook District."

t_ch: 鸽川区有什么真正的区容区貌可言吗？他们放着共愿帮不管，却来管这些小事情啵。
t_en: Public image? Does Dovebrook District even have one? They let the Synwish Syndicate run wild, but get bent out of shape over trivial matters like this.

t_ch: 关于那位睡在隔壁的侦探…
t_en: Tell me more about the detective next door...

t_ch: 那位侦探，比虚照大人还神秘，本狸都不敢记住他的正脸，怕被他杀狸灭…不过，我也没机会记住，大部分时候他都在棺材里睡觉。
t_en: That detective... he's even more enigmatic than Lady Nihilux. I wouldn't dare to remember what he looks like, out of fear of being hushed... Then again, I've never really had the opportunity. He's usually asleep in his coffin.

t_ch: 幸好，他白天睡觉，晚上干活，和我们的工作日程不冲突啵！
t_en: Fortunately, he sleeps all day and works all night, so our schedules don't overlap!

t_ch: 加油！把杂志社干倒闭。
t_en: You can do it! Drive the publishing house out of business.

t_ch: 哔哔！加油…不对！不能倒闭，杂志社红红火火，我们才能顿顿吃上油豆腐啵！
t_en: Zip-zip! Yeah, I can do it... No, wait! It can't go out of business! The publishing house has to be a success, or we won't get to eat fried tofu all the time!

t_ch: 在二相乐园，任何幻造种都可以成任何事！你也给我变——
t_en: In Planarcadia, any imagenae can be whatever they want! Now, it's your turn to transform!

t_ch: 进步这么大？报辅导班了？
t_en: You made so much progress? Did you get a tutor?

t_ch: 我爸在匹诺康尼教过我…
t_en: My dad taught me in Penacony...

t_ch: 真是一代不如一代…
t_en: Kids these days just ain't what their parents used to be...

t_ch: 我年轻的时候啊…
t_en: When I was younger...

t_ch: 可是亲自上房揭瓦的！
t_en: I climbed up on the roof and everything!

t_ch: 啾啾啾~
t_en: Tweet tweet tweet~

t_ch: 我可不是折纸小鸟啾！
t_en: I'm not some Origami Bird, tweet!

t_ch: 我画了支笔替我画画！
t_en: I drew a pen to do the drawing for me!

t_ch: 可它比我还懒，所以…
t_en: But it's even lazier than me, so...

t_ch: 它又画了支笔替它画！
t_en: It drew another pen to do the drawing for itself!

t_ch: 我都要爱上啵啵娃老师了！
t_en: I'm falling head over heels for Ms. Dollie!

t_ch: 那你可得当心了…
t_en: Then you better watch out...

t_ch: 再留级一年，我就…
t_en: If I have to repeat a year again, I'm...

t_ch: 要被退学？
t_en: Getting expelled?

t_ch: 不，就有专属学号了！
t_en: No, I'm going to get an exclusive student ID number!

t_ch: 又是信？这周第几封了？
t_en: Another letter? How many is that this week?

t_ch: 你应该庆幸自己没收到…
t_en: You should be grateful you didn't get one...

t_ch: 描边要再粗两毫米。
t_en: Make the outline two millimeters thicker.

t_ch: 你真的懂画画吗…
t_en: Do you even know how to draw...

t_ch: 跟你讲个八卦…
t_en: Psst... got some juicy gossip...

t_ch: 周围应该安全…吧？
t_en: Should be safe around here... Right?

t_ch: 秘密，喜欢秘密。
t_en: Secrets, I love secrets.

t_ch: 有同学借幻造饵忘了还。
t_en: Someone borrowed imagenated bait and forgot to return it.

t_ch: 结果尘灵把教室淹了。
t_en: And then the wispae flooded the classroom.

t_ch: 那还真…挺想看看的！
t_en: Oh, I'd actually... like to see that!

t_ch: 我懂了，是我，杀了我！
t_en: I get it now. It was me! Kill me!

t_ch: 你基本可以告别推理了…
t_en: You can pretty much kiss deduction goodbye...

t_ch: 陪我去购买部吧！求你！
t_en: Come shopping with me! Pretty please!

t_ch: 没有你我都挤不进人群…
t_en: I couldn't even squeeze through the crowd without you...

t_ch: 好，等买到分我一半。
t_en: Alright, when you get it, split it with me fifty-fifty.

t_ch: 我枯了，谁来教我画画…
t_en: I'm so done... Can someone teach me how to draw...

t_ch: 我瞧瞧…还挺好看的嘛！
t_en: Let me see... Not bad at all!

t_ch: 要睡回家去睡！
t_en: Go home if you wanna sleep!

t_ch: 其实我最近失眠…
t_en: I've been having trouble sleeping lately...

t_ch: 要听你的课才睡得着。
t_en: Your lectures are better than sleeping pills.

t_ch: 我要代表社团出战…
t_en: I'll be representing my club...

t_ch: 参加下个月的校运会！
t_en: In next month's School Sports Day!

t_ch: 恭喜！是哪个社团来着？

t_ch: 站外引导图。它放在这里的目的，用正式的说法是：引导乘客前往站外目的地。用不正式的说法就是：请你速速离开。
t_en: A station guide map. Officially, its purpose is to direct passengers to their off-site destinations. Unofficially, It's basically telling you: "Please see yourself out. Promptly."

t_ch: 站外引导图会告诉你车站附近的主要建筑物、商场、学校、医院、公交枢纽、景点…但是它不会告诉你的是：我在泊地站很想你。
t_en: The station guide map will show you the major buildings, malls, schools, hospitals, transit hubs, attractions nearby... but what it won't tell you is: I really miss you here at Perdido Station.

t_ch: 突如其来的幻造种小百科——名字：疾锋龙；属性：电、龙；特性：幻造引擎；图鉴颜色：紫色；种族值总和：<unbreak>670</unbreak>。有着「认真」的性格。
t_en: Out of nowhere, imagenae mini-encyclopedia — Name: Bullet Wyrm. Type: Electric / Dragon. Trait: Imagenated Engine; Index Color: Purple. Base Stat Total: <unbreak>670</unbreak>. Has a Serious nature.

t_ch: 在遍布幻造的世界里，哪怕真实与虚构没有明确的界限…探索还是有「边界感」的。
t_en: In a world filled with imagenae, even if there's no clear boundary between reality and fiction... exploration still has its "limits."

t_ch: 「地铁传媒，广告招募。」
t_en: "Metro Media: Advertising Slots Available."

t_ch: 激烈的撞色、富有张力的构图，夸张的速度线特效，共同营造出一种真实动感的视觉效果——它竟然只是一个空位待租的招商广告？
t_en: Bold color contrasts, dynamic composition, exaggerated speed-line effects, all creating this intense, kinetic visual impact. And yet it's just an ad for a vacant rental slot?

t_ch: 「快速、安全、便捷、环保——欢迎乘坐全银河最安全的疾锋龙列车」。显然使用「最」字违反了广告法要求，再说了，星穹列车的帕姆肯定不会同意……
t_en: "Fast, safe, convenient, and eco-friendly: Welcome aboard the safest express in the galaxy, the Bullet Wyrm Express." Obviously using the word "safest" violates advertising regulations, and besides, Pom-Pom from the Astral Express would definitely disagree...

t_ch: 但说真的，星穹列车的运行方式真的安全吗？这里，我们不妨留下一个悬念。
t_en: But seriously, is the way the Astral Express operates really safe? Well, let's leave that question hanging.

t_ch: 某些狂热的疾锋龙粉丝会对着墙上的海报痴笑，你可千万不要这样做啊。
t_en: Some overzealous Bullet Wyrm fans grin like idiots at posters on their walls. Whatever you do, don't you ever do that.

t_ch: 克拉肯名下的餐厅分店，鲜花、画架和颜料罐为这里平添了一丝高雅的艺术气息。
t_en: A branch of the Kraken restaurant chain. The flowers, easels, and paint cans lend the place a sophisticated, artistic vibe.

t_ch: 在「手机先吃」的时代，相比餐品本身，精致的摆盘和浪漫的环境似乎才更能满足摄像头的胃口。
t_en: In this "cameras eat first" age, fancy plating and a romantic setting seem more likely to satisfy a phone's appetite than the actual meal.

t_ch: 触目可及都是高雅的艺术作品，等待高雅人士品鉴。
t_en: Sophisticated works of art fill the view, just waiting for refined individuals to come and admire them.

t_ch: 海报的滚动频率被精准地调试过——让人刚好不能完整阅读一页内容，所以有时候为了看完海报的信息，不得不把所有海报都浏览一遍。
t_en: The scrolling speed of the posters has been precisely calibrated, just fast enough that you can't read a full page, so sometimes you have to go through all the posters just to catch the complete information.

t_ch: 一般来说，投放广告的位置要与目标用户高度重合，那么…玩具广告、游戏广告、甜品店的广告都在这里，它的目标用户究竟是谁？
t_en: Usually, ads are placed where they overlap heavily with the target audience, so... ads for toys, games, and desserts are all here. Just who exactly is their target demographic?

t_ch: 据说现代人类每天要接收<unbreak>5000</unbreak>条以上的广告，所以…你完成了今日目标吗？
t_en: They say modern humans are exposed to over <unbreak>5,000</unbreak> ads every day, so... have you hit your daily quota yet?

t_ch: 星穹列车俨然成为了二相乐园最热门的网红打卡点，或许，列车长该考虑收费参观了…你默默在星穹列车的账本上又记下了一笔。
t_en: The Astral Express has become Planarcadia's hottest viral tourist spot. Perhaps the conductor should consider charging for tours... You silently add another entry to the Astral Express's ledger.

t_ch: 这些家伙是怎么爬上这么高的车头的，还在上面乱踩乱跑，不知道去一趟洗车星要花多少信用点吗？你默默在星穹列车的账本上又记下了一笔。
t_en: How did these guys manage to climb up on such a high locomotive, running and stomping all over it? Don't they know how many credits a trip to Washtopia costs? You silently add another entry to the Astral Express's ledger.

t_ch: 看着星穹列车，你还记得踏上旅途那天的回忆——小三月趴在车窗上，向黑塔空间站的朋友们告别，而你也收获了全新的身份。
t_en: Looking at the Astral Express, you recall the day your journey began: March pressed against the window, bidding farewell to her friends at the Herta Space Station, while you also stepped into a brand-new identity.

t_ch: 「Oh～We will see，Come with me take the journey～」
t_en: "Oh~ We will see, come with me take the journey~"

t_ch: 「可燃垃圾、有害垃圾、可回收垃圾」。你为标准众多的垃圾分类感到疑惑。
t_en: "Combustibles, hazardous waste, recyclables." You're puzzled by the numerous waste sorting standards.

t_ch: 无论是各自有着怎样的面貌与经历，在严格的垃圾桶分类面前，都会自己找到入口。
t_en: No matter what appearances and experiences one has, when it comes to strict waste sorting, everyone will find their own way in.

t_ch: 我的国王，自你抵达二维市的那一天起，整个垃圾桶界都在低语着你的名字：{NICKNAME}。
t_en: My king, since the day you arrived in Duomension City, your name has been whispered throughout the trashcan realm: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你注意到一个特别的分类提示：不好笑的笑话也是不可燃的垃圾…真是太贴心了。
t_en: You notice a special sorting hint: "Unfunny jokes are also non-combustible waste"... How thoughtful!

t_ch: 看见垃圾桶总是有一种亲近感，就像是在异国他乡见到了老友，即便它已经是别的形状了。
t_en: Seeing a trashcan can always inspire a sense of familiarity, like running into an old friend in a foreign land, even if it looks different now.

t_ch: 你注意到了，明明是科技如此发达的二相乐园，在很多小地方却尤其复古…比如说这种款式的自行车，说不定已经有数百年的历史了。
t_en: You've noticed it too. Despite being so technologically advanced, Planarcadia is surprisingly retro in details... Like this style of bicycle, which probably has hundreds of years of history behind it.

t_ch: 最朴实无华的交通工具，绝对节能环保，百公里只需消耗两个面包（仅理论值）。
t_en: The most down-to-earth mode of transportation, absolutely eco-friendly and energy-efficient. It only consumes two breads per <unbreak>100</unbreak> kilometers (theoretical value only).

t_ch: 曾有部电影作品讲述了，一个小孩和特殊幻造种骑着自行车，飞到了幻月上——电影大热之后，为了避免市民效仿危险驾驶，全面取消了前车筐。
t_en: There was once a movie about a kid and a special imagenae who rode a bicycle and flew to Phantasmoon. After the film became a huge hit, front bike baskets were completely banned to prevent citizens from attempting such dangerous riding.

t_ch: 嘤嘤嘤~在遍布幻造的世界里，哪怕真实与虚构没有明确的界限…探索还是有「边界感」的，不可以继续往前咯～
t_en: Snrk-snrk~ In a world filled with imagenae, even if there's no clear boundary between reality and fiction... exploration still has its "limits," so we can't go any further~

t_ch: 这干净的水口、光泽的漆面、严丝合缝的合模线，简直像从动画里直接开出来的…已经能够猜到杨叔走不动道的理由了。
t_en: The pristine water inlet, the glossy finish, the flawless mold lines, it looks like it came straight out of an cartoon... I can already tell why Mr. Yang can't move.

t_ch: 「请勿触摸机铠，任何损害公司资产的行为都需照价赔偿」。你看了看这些精美的包装盒，上面没有标价，是不是意味着摸了也是零赔偿？
t_en: "Do not touch the mechatron. Any damage to IPC assets will require full compensation." You glance at these exquisite display boxes. There's no price tag on them. Does that mean touching them equals zero compensation?

t_ch: 不同的机铠将头部与身体分离，然后头部会接上新的身体…合理推测，机铠脖子（如果那里能被称为「脖子」）以下的部分属于公共租赁范畴。
t_en: Different mechatrons can separate their head from their body, then attach the head to a new body... It's reasonable to assume that everything below the mechatron's neck (if you can even call it a "neck") falls under public rental territory.

t_ch: 机铠，屹立于大地之上。曾经机铠被当作是公司带来的异星文化…而现在，它已经是能够代表二相乐园的文化符号了——时代总是在不停地变化。
t_en: The mechatron stands tall upon the Earth. Once seen as an alien culture brought by the IPC... now it has become a cultural symbol that represents Planarcadia. Times are always changing.

t_ch: 你与二次元的世界，仅仅只隔了一层玻璃。只要、只要能够打破这最后的阻碍，你…就会被得到蓄意破坏财产相关的处罚。
t_en: The cartoon world is just a pane of glass away from you. If only... if only you could break through this final barrier, you'd... get charged with intentional property damage.

t_ch: 橱窗中的漫画角色来自《我们仍未知道那天于群星熄灭之时在熵增尽头歌唱的愚者与他那永无止境的最后一程忘记过去只记得自己是招财猫》。
t_en: The comic character in the display window is from We Still Don't Know What Happened When the Stars Went Dark That Day as the Fool Sings at the End of Entropy on His Endless Final Journey Forgetting the Past Only Remembering He's a Lucky Cat.

t_ch: 希望催更《苍天航路绒绒号》的粉丝能够搞清楚，这位「招财猫」不是模糊二维马老师。模糊二维马老师要是有招财的本事，她不会画漫画。
t_en: Fans pestering for Fluffy Across the Blue updates should realize this "fortune cat" isn't Blurry_QR_Colt. If she actually had the power to bring in money, she wouldn't be a comic artist.

t_ch: 随着黑白漫画向彩色漫画转型，这家漫画店也迎来了第二增长曲线。事实证明，人类的目光还是更容易被彩色吸引。
t_en: With the shift from black-and-white to color comics, this shop found its second wind, a proof that humans are naturally drawn to a bit of color.

t_ch: 大吃一惊！大惑不解！大惊失色！这是「BOOK-ON！」的三大经营宗旨，意在为读者带来意料之外的情感体验。
t_en: Shock! Confusion! Astonishment! These are the three pillars of BOOK-ON!, all designed to give readers an emotional experience they never saw coming.

t_ch: 招牌使用了特殊光栅工艺，转换角度便能看出裸眼景深效果。所以在招牌前摇头晃脑的游客并不是脖子不舒服。
t_en: This sign uses a special light barrier technique to create a 3D effect without glasses. So don't worry, the tourists bobbing their heads in front of it aren't having neck spasms.

t_ch: 「本店隆重推出诺布莱斯虫粘液特调，欢愉您的味蕾！」，听起来是可以和苏打豆汁儿坐一桌的饮品。
t_en: "We proudly present our special blend of Noblesse Worm mucus, a delight for your taste buds!" Sounds like something that can be placed on the same table with mung bean soda.

t_ch: 海报下面写这一行警示语：「禁止偷吃海报上的饮品！」看起来二维化的幻造种，有自己独特的觅食技巧。
t_en: Below the poster, there's a warning that reads: "DO NOT drink the beverages shown on this poster!" Looks like these 2D imagenae have their own unique feeding habits.

t_ch: 这家咖啡店的饮品都绿绿的，和你认知中的「咖啡」相去甚远。但如果这是姬子理解中的咖啡，那很多事就说得通了。
t_en: All the drinks in this coffee shop are green, far from what you'd normally think of as "coffee." But if this is what Himeko considers coffee, then a lot of things suddenly make sense.

t_ch: 二相乐园的人们非常热爱排队。也许因为排长队才得到的东西，会让人有一种历尽千帆的满足感吧。
t_en: The people of Planarcadia really love queuing. Maybe it's because getting something after waiting in a long line gives you that satisfying feeling of having gone through an epic journey.

t_ch: 人为什么要排队？因为队就在那里。
t_en: Why do people stand in line? Because the line is right there.

t_ch: 队伍缓慢行进，似乎永无尽头。搞不好这些正在排队的人，都已经不知道自己在排什么了。
t_en: The line inches forward at a snail's pace, seemingly endless. For all we know, these people in line might've already forgotten what they're even queuing for.

t_ch: 景观设计师计划用四季常青的绿树装点河岸。当他被确诊色盲时一切都已经晚了。
t_en: The landscape architect intended to line the river with evergreens. Unfortunately, by the time his color blindness was diagnosed, it was too late to change anything.

t_ch: 眼前的几棵树在「二相乐园年度风云树木」评比中包揽了前三名。
t_en: These trees right here took the top three spots in the Planarcadia Annual Tree of the Year awards.

t_ch: 灿黄的落叶缓缓飘落，像思念。
t_en: Bright yellow leaves drift down slowly, like memories of someone far away.

t_ch: 在二相乐园，商家并不需要费心回收这些玻璃瓶交还给供应商，它们会自己回到指定的箱子中。幻造艺术，很神奇吧？
t_en: In Planarcadia, shopkeepers don't need to bother returning glass bottles to suppliers, for they magically find their way back to designated boxes on their own. Imagenesis, pretty neat, huh?

t_ch: 每到幻月游戏期间，各类汽水饮料的需求量总是会暴增。看起来大家都喜欢一边喝汽水一边看热闹。商家也会抓住这数年一遇的良机，推出各种促销活动。
t_en: During the Phantasmoon Games, the demand for all kinds of carbonated drinks always skyrockets. Seems like everyone loves to have soda while watching the festivities. Merchants also seize this rare opportunity that comes only once every few years, rolling out various promotional campaigns.

t_ch: 前些年，曾有玻璃瓶团结起来争取劳动权益。因此现在饮料厂商大幅改进了饮料箱的设计，确保每个玻璃瓶都能在里面居住得舒心。
t_en: A few years ago, glass bottles united in solidarity to fight for their labor rights. As a result, beverage manufacturers have greatly improved their crate designs to ensure every glass bottle can live comfortably inside.

t_ch: 「我们不生产家具，我们只是家具的搬运工。」海报上如此写道，其实你对它也没有更多的期待。
t_en: "We don't make furniture. We just move it." So says the poster, and you honestly didn't expect much more than that.

t_ch: 要添购些家具吗？星穹列车在天上期待地看着你。
t_en: Thinking of buying some furniture? The Astral Express is watching you from above with high hopes.

t_ch: 听说有人在家具城里能够以一挡百。如果有机会，你不介意也试试。
t_en: Word is someone in the furniture store can single-handedly take on a hundred opponents. If the opportunity arises, you wouldn't mind putting that to the test.

t_ch: 该路段正在紧急修复中，修复时间未定，请千万不要耐心等待。
t_en: This section is under emergency repairs with no estimated time of completion. Please do NOT wait around!

t_ch: 有些人说「世上没有错误的道路」。他们错了，显然有。这不就是吗？它都报错了。
t_en: Some people say, "there are no wrong paths in the world." They're wrong, and obviously there are. Just look at this one, an Error Pop-up on the road.

t_ch: 拦住你道路的是名为「错误弹窗」的幻造种。这一种族数量稀少、沉默寡言、社会职能单一，很多人都不知道它们竟然也是智慧生命。
t_en: Blocking your path is an imagenae known as "Error Pop-up." This rare species is quiet and reserved, with a singular social function, so much so that many don't even realize they're sentient beings.

t_ch: 温馨提示：在二相乐园，随意询问店家「这里是否销售猫屎咖啡？」有可能构成「骚扰罪」，最严重可被判处6个月社区劳动。如果你一定想问，请确保店员不是轻脚族。
t_en: A friendly reminder: Asking for "civet coffee" in Planarcadia might count as harassment, potentially earning you six months of community service in severe cases. If you absolutely have to ask, make sure the staff member isn't a lightpaw.

t_ch: 二相乐园的「咖啡」并非依靠「咖啡因」，而是依靠其中「想要清醒」的愿望。采集这种愿望，会导致被采集愿望的幻造种赖床不起。很残忍吧？没有买卖就没有赖床。
t_en: The "coffee" in Planarcadia doesn't contain "caffeine," but rather the desire to "stay awake" within it. Harvesting this desire causes the imagenae whose wishes are harvested to oversleep. Cruel, isn't it? No trading, no oversleeping.

t_ch: 二相乐园的「咖啡」与银河社会普遍理解中的「咖啡」根本不是同一种饮品，只是口感相似而已。而这里正是列车咖啡杀手姬子出生长大的地方…哦…你逐渐理解了一切。
t_en: The "coffee" in Planarcadia is a completely different drink from what's commonly known as "coffee" in the cosmic society. They just happen to taste similar. And this is where Himeko, the Express Coffee Killer, was born and raised... Oh... now you're starting to understand everything.

t_ch: 「伊磨吉」也是一种「文字」。只不过它们不负责传达任何具体的信息，而专门被用于传达情绪。
t_en: "Emotis" are also a form of text. They just don't convey any specific information, but are purely used to convey emotions.

t_ch: 这些的对话框的飞行速度相当快，因此也被当地人称为「弹幕」。二维市的医院每年都会收治不少被「弹幕」撞伤的患者。
t_en: These dialogue boxes fly at quite a high speed, which is why locals call them "bullet comments." Duomension City's hospitals treat numerous patients knocked and injured by them every year.

t_ch: 在二相乐园，人们在交流中越来越依赖于「伊磨吉」。年轻人更倾向于进行纯粹的情绪表达，因为已经很难再靠讨论具体的信息得出任何共识了。
t_en: In Planarcadia, people have become increasingly reliant on "emotis" to communicate. Young people tend to express pure emotions, as it has become nearly impossible to reach any consensus through discussing concrete information.

t_ch: 前方并没有任何事物正在被加载。这个转来转去的圈圈只是装饰。
t_en: Nothing is actually loading ahead. This spinning circle is just for decoration.

t_ch: 据说盯着这个转来转去的加载动画，会被催眠。仔细盯着它看，你会感到自己很困…很困…很困…然后，你会感到自己充满自信、无所不能、永远不会被困难打败！
t_en: They say staring at this spinning loading animation can hypnotize you. Keep your eyes on it, and you'll feel sleepy... sleepy... sleepy... and then, you'll feel full of confidence, omnipotent, and invincible!

t_ch: 很好奇这背后藏着什么？请相信，这里面什么东西都没有。至少没有你会感兴趣的事物。
t_en: Curious about what's hidden behind this? Trust me, there's nothing in here. At least nothing you'd be interested in.

t_ch: 绘制着「怪盗变化狸」的海报。追随者眼中济富济贫的侠盗，治安官案卷中的愿力小偷。
t_en: A poster of the Phantom Furbomorph. A heroic rogue to their fans, but just another Wishpower thief to the security officers.

t_ch: 怪盗变化狸虽已不在江湖，但江湖依然流传着它们的传说。
t_en: The Phantom Furbomorph may have retired from the world, but its legend lives on.

t_ch: 怪盗变化狸在作案时真的会摆出如此摇滚的手势吗？至少它们的粉丝是如此坚信着。
t_en: Do they really strike such rock-and-roll poses while on the job? Their fans certainly seem to think so.

t_ch: 得意的伊磨吉
t_en: Smug Emoti

t_ch: 你看出来了吗？我是幻月同款，请叫我「小月月」。
t_en: Can't you tell? I'm styled after the Phantasmoon itself. You can call me "Moonie."

t_ch: 干脆我也办个「小幻月游戏」吧，胜者可以成为一分钟的伊磨吉。
t_en: Why don't I just host my own "Mini Phantasmoon Games"? The winner gets to be an emoti for a whole minute.

t_ch: 施法的伊磨吉
t_en: Spellcasting Emoti

t_ch: 变傻光线——
t_en: Stupefying Ray!

t_ch: 在二相乐园，任何幻造种都可以成任何事！你也给我变——
t_en: In Planarcadia, any imagenae can be whatever they want! Now, it's your turn to transform!

t_ch: 狸狸通信杂志社的狸猫有一个习惯——即便办公室有一台二手咖啡机，但是大家还是会下单点外送的咖啡，到底是为什么呢？
t_en: The furbos at Furbobocom Magazine have a weird quirk. Even with a perfectly good second-hand coffee maker in the office, they still insist on ordering delivery. I wonder why?

t_ch: 校园公告栏
t_en: Campus Bulletin Board

t_ch: 「欢迎收听校园杂谈频道♪我是你们亲爱的广播先生——」热情的嗓音伴随每次课间铃声准时响起，回荡在室内每个角落，「接下来是最新校园消息……」
t_en: "Welcome to Campus Talk Radio ♪ This is your beloved Radio Host..." The enthusiastic voice rings punctually with every class break bell, echoing through every corner of the room, "And now for the latest campus news..."

t_ch: 作为公告栏主持人，广播先生有着无可摘指的工作热情与素养；但很可惜，很多事情并不是只要努力就够了——音量槽上大大小小、深浅不一的指印早已说明了一切。
t_en: As the bulletin board host, Mr. Broadcast had an impeccable work ethic and professionalism. Unfortunately, effort alone isn't always enough, as evidenced by the countless finger marks of varying sizes and depths on the volume slider.

t_ch: 「广播先生有三好：声大、话痨、关不掉。」
t_en: "Mr. Broadcast has three wonderful traits: Loud, chatty, and impossible to turn off."

t_ch: 电视妹妹
t_en: Ms. Telly

t_ch: 该公告栏常以新闻形式，播报校内重大消息，包括但不限于：活动通知、批评通报、失物招领，奖学金名单……
t_en: The bulletin board regularly broadcasts important campus news in a newsletter format, including but not limited to: Event announcements, disciplinary notices, lost and found items, scholarship recipient lists...

t_ch: 这个公告栏常年由「电视妹妹」负责播报。全校师生都很喜欢「电视妹妹」，但竟然没有一个人记得她是何时入职的。
t_en: This bulletin board has always been managed by Ms. Telly. Everyone at school loves her, but surprisingly, no one remembers when she was actually hired.

t_ch: 许是你的目光在屏幕上驻留太久，大厅上空的纸鹤们突然纷纷转向你，随即带着电流的声音幽幽响起：「你…想上电视吗？」
t_en: Perhaps because your gaze lingered too long on the screen, the paper cranes floating above the hall suddenly all turned to face you. Then, with an eerie electrical crackle, they spoke: "Would you... like to be on TV?"

t_ch: 饮水机
t_en: Drinking Station

t_ch: 当你看到这段话，请提醒自己去喝杯水。记得不要总喝含糖饮料哦。
t_en: When you see this message, remember to drink some water. And don't forget, try not to drink too many sugary beverages!

t_ch: 饮水机上的眼睛滴溜溜地转动着。它是绘世学院的健康饮水监督员。
t_en: The eyes on the drinking station swiveled around playfully. It's the healthy hydration monitor of Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 水灵灵的眼睛滴溜溜地转动着。这可能是你见过最水灵灵的眼睛。
t_en: The bright, sparkling eyes swiveled around playfully. These might be the most crystal-clear, lively eyes you've ever seen.

t_ch: 七步洗手法
t_en: Seven-Step Hand Washing Method

t_ch: 七步洗手法：第一步，揉搓；第二步，交叠揉搓；第三步，缠绕交叠揉搓；第…好吧，你编不下去了——这不就是两条触手扭来扭去相互缠绕吗！
t_en: Seven-step hand washing: Step 1, rub. Step 2, overlap and rub. Step 3, intertwine, overlap and rub. Step... oh forget it. You can't make anything else up. Anyway, it's just two tentacles twisting and wrapping around each other!

t_ch: 你不知道它究竟有没有洗干净，但你知道自己现在很饿，想吃海鲜。
t_en: You're not sure if it's properly cleaned, but you know you're hungry and craving seafood.

t_ch: 或许对于某些希望回归大海的人而言，这幅海报上的画面堪称赏心悦目——但很可惜，你并不在此列。
t_en: For some individuals keen on returning to the ocean, this poster might be a feast for the eyes, but unfortunately, you're not one of them.

t_ch: 姬子像
t_en: Statue of Himeko

t_ch: 绘世学院优秀毕业生——姬子。
t_en: Himeko, Outstanding Graduate of the Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 这是姬子小时候的样子？她真好看。
t_en: Is this what Himeko looked like when she was young? She's so pretty.

t_ch: 哇！姬子竟然还有这么年轻的时候？总感觉她从一出生开始就是可靠的领航员了。
t_en: Wow! So Himeko was actually this young once? I always thought she was born as a reliable Navigator!

t_ch: 姬子小姐年轻时，果然也是飒爽的模样。
t_en: Miss Himeko must have been quite stylish in her younger days.

t_ch: 年轻时的姬子啊…唉，突然有些莫名地怀念起…不，虽然容貌相似，也同样是了不起的人物，但那并不是她，不可混淆。
t_en: Young Himeko... *sigh* I suddenly feel rather nostalgic... No, although they look similar and are both remarkable people, that's not her. I mustn't confuse them.

t_ch: 啊…这东西怎么还在？
t_en: Ah... why is this thing still here?

t_ch: 星穹列车的领航员，似乎早在青葱岁月就是个光彩夺目的人。
t_en: The Navigator of the Astral Express seems to have been a dazzling figure since her youth.

t_ch: 真珠画像
t_en: Pearl's Portrait

t_ch: 若是你坐上了乐园首席执行官的位置，你也可以在不同学校的校长办公室中有一幅自己的画像。
t_en: If you ever became the CEO of Arcadia, you'd probably have your portrait hanging in every principal's office, too.

t_ch: 名人名言语录
t_en: Famous Quotes

t_ch: 别吃我，我保证不拖稿了还不行吗？！ ——小田田•走路菇
t_en: Don't eat me! I'll stop missing deadlines, alright!? — Paddy the Walking Mushroom

t_ch: 任何照片挂在这里，都像是名人；任何句子写在这里，都像是名言。——无名氏
t_en: Any photo hung here looks like a celebrity portrait. Any sentence written here sounds like a famous quote. — Nameless

t_ch: 荣誉展示墙
t_en: Wall of Honor

t_ch: 此处怕是全校最地位分明的地方——最大的金杯站C位，银杯负责侧光补亮，奖状与照片循规蹈矩地蜗居相框里，无声充当着背景板。
t_en: This must be the place with the clearest hierarchy in the whole school: The biggest gold trophy commands the center stage, the silver trophy provides supporting lighting from the sides, while certificates and photos dutifully huddle in their frames, silently serving as the backdrop.

t_ch: 对周围的异样视而不见，此乃成熟的标志——别误会，说的是那边的奖杯。
t_en: Turning a blind eye to oddities around you is a sign of maturity. Don't get me wrong, I'm talking about that trophy over there.

t_ch: 请听题——什么东西既在这里，又不在这里？锵锵～答案当然是——「桌上量子奖杯」！这种家中常备妙妙道具，应该很容易想到吧？
t_en: Pop quiz time! What's something that's both here and not here? Ta-da~ The answer is, of course, the "Quantum Trophy on the Table"! It's such a common household gadget, you must've figured it out right away, right?

t_ch: 档案
t_en: Archives

t_ch: 除去普通的行政管理、学术科研、学生信息等，还收录各种版本的校园传说。但这不是重点，重点是：这些档案都是活的，偶尔还会去老饕小巷团建。
t_en: Besides the usual administrative stuff, academic research, and student info, it also contains various versions of campus urban legends. But that's not the kicker. Here's the real deal: These files are alive, and they occasionally go for team outings in Yum Yum Alley!

t_ch: 某些特殊档案的查询遵循「等价交换」原则：比如当试图查找某位老师的黑历史时，你的挂科成绩单有概率出现在校园公告栏上——请谨慎借阅。
t_en: Accessing certain special files follows the "Quid Pro Quo" principle. For instance, when attempting to dig up dirt on a professor, your failed grades might mysteriously appear on the campus notice board. Please borrow with caution.

t_ch: 据说其中大部分档案都由旁边自称名侦探的*被诅咒之物*「青蛙白板」整理而成——某种意义上，比起名侦探，档案管理员这个职业似乎更适合他。
t_en: Rumor has it that most of these archives were organized by the self-proclaimed detective, cursed object Froggy Whiteboard, nearby. In a sense, an archive administrator might be a more fitting path for him than a detective.

t_ch: 楼梯鬼影
t_en: Stairway Shadow

t_ch: 咯咯咯，你听说过「细长鬼影」吗……
t_en: Hehehe... have you heard the legend of the "Slender Man"?

t_ch: 覆世沧浪图
t_en: Worldbreaker Waves

t_ch: 《覆世沧浪图》，绘世的遗作。她就是历史上，「幻造」出一个完整的位面，将整个二相乐园收入画中的绘师。
t_en: Worldbreaker Waves, the final masterpiece by Graphia. She's the legendary artist who managed to imagenate an entire dimension, capturing all of Planarcadia within her work.

t_ch: 「引鸽川之水抹消错笔，不意呈浪花之形，遂挥毫作此景画，以诠物形之妙。」
t_en: "While using Dovebrook water to erase a mistake, it unexpectedly formed the shape of waves, so I wielded my brush to create this scene, illustrating the wonders of natural forms."

t_ch: 每届新生入学时，总被叮嘱要对着最高的浪头行礼——据说曾有位学长虔诚一拜，第二天庭中浪花就洒到试卷上，帮他答对了三道选择题。
t_en: Every year, freshmen are told to bow to the highest wave. Legend has it that when a senior student once made a sincere bow, water droplets from the courtyard splattered onto his exam paper the next day, helping him get three multiple-choice questions right.

t_ch: 学力测试之眼
t_en: Eye of Academic Assessment

t_ch: 作为专业的「学力测试之眼」，其通常能精确测试出学生资质与成绩变化趋势，出具《学习潜力报告》。但偶尔会将通宵打游戏的亢奋误判成了学习潜力。
t_en: As a professional-grade Eye of Academic Assessment, it can usually accurately measure students' aptitude and academic performance trends, producing Academic Potential Reports. However, it occasionally misinterprets the excitement from pulling all-nighters gaming as learning potential.

t_ch: 镜片具有扫描功能，能够实时读取学生的脑电波，锁定每个在课堂上开小差的家伙，并在镜片上投影其姓名学号。
t_en: The lenses have a scanning function that can read students' brainwaves in real-time, lock on to anyone daydreaming in class, and display their names and student IDs right on the lens display.

t_ch: 学生间流传的反测试秘诀：考前对着它疯狂眨眼，《学习潜力报告》会自动生成「此子必成大器」或「此子必成大患」…但这样的行为，只不过是掩耳盗铃罢了。
t_en: There's this anti-test "hack" that students pass around: Frantically blink at it before exams, and the Academic Potential Report will automatically generate either "This one's destined for greatness" or "This one's destined for trouble"... but that's just burying their heads in the sand.

t_ch: 巅峰王座
t_en: Apical Throne

t_ch: 二相乐园的每所高校都存在这种「不良少年之顶点」的王座。但绘世学院的校风很好，所以顶点的争霸战烈度也很低。
t_en: Every school in Planarcadia has a throne for the "King of Delinquents." But since Graphia Academy has such a good school culture, the fights for the seat aren't exactly intense.

t_ch: 据说学生间会定期举办擂台赛，以争夺该王座的使用权——人称：「王座保卫战」。而对应的擂主，也被称为「王座级强者」。
t_en: Word has it that students regularly hold tournaments to compete for the right to use this throne. They call it the "Throne Defense Wars," and the winner is crowned a "throne-tier warrior."

t_ch: 校园论坛上有一则「巅峰王座使用指南」，核心内容是教人如何用体操动作从摇晃的椅子上安全着陆——比物理课受力分析实用多了。
t_en: There's a Apical Throne Manual on the campus forum, mainly teaching readers how to safely land from a rocking chair using gymnastics moves. Way more practical than force analysis in physics class.

t_ch: 学生书法展
t_en: Student Calligraphy Exhibition

t_ch: 校方为了鼓励学生全面发展、充分培养个人兴趣所举办的活动之一。但据说比起书法，参与者们更感兴趣的其实是报名后奖励的学分。
t_en: One of the activities organized by the school to encourage students' well-rounded development and cultivate their personal interests. However, it's said that participants were more interested in the course credits awarded after registration than the actual calligraphy.

t_ch: 有些书法作品或许需要借助想象力，才能知道上面究竟写了什么。你只觉得它们看起来像是鬼画符，或者心电图。
t_en: You might require some imagination to figure out what's actually written on some of these calligraphy works. They look like paranormal scrawling or an electrocardiogram reading to you.

t_ch: 你很好奇：其中有多少笔走龙蛇的作品，其实是学生们交作业前一晚疯狂赶稿的产物。
t_en: You wonder: how many of those masterful strokes were actually the results of students' frantic, last-minute cram on the night before assignments were due.

t_ch: 小舞台
t_en: Mini Stage

t_ch: 「有一条狗来到酒馆，说：『我什么也看不见。我猜我要打开这个。』」这是人类最古老的笑话之一，但已经没有人知道它好笑在哪里了。（推测可能是谐音梗或双关语。）
t_en: "A dog walks into a tavern and says, 'I can't see anything. Guess I should open this.'" It's said to be one of the oldest human jokes, but no one remembers why it was funny anymore. (Presumably it involved a pun or wordplay.)

t_ch: 现在，来试试创作符合这个时代的笑话吧！请在空白处填写你认为好笑的回答：「有一条狗来到酒馆，说：『我什么也看不见。』酒保说：『_______________』」
t_en: Now, let's try creating a joke that fits this era! Fill in the blank with what you think would be funny: "A dog walks into a tavern and says: 'I can't see anything.' The drinksmith goes: '_______________'"

t_ch: 「有一条狗来到酒馆，说：『我什么也看不见。』酒保说：『我去！这条狗怎么会说话！』」好吧，这个应该没有你想出来的好笑。
t_en: "A dog walks into a tavern and says, 'I can't see anything.' The drinksmith goes, 'Holy cow! A talking dog!'" Yeah, yours will probably be way funnier than that.

t_ch: 酒窖
t_en: Wine Cellar

t_ch: 这些酒桶中的佳酿正在发酵中，现在还只是酸涩的果汁。
t_en: The wine in these barrels is still fermenting. Right now, it's just sour fruit juice.

t_ch: 愚者们每天都要对着这些酒桶讲笑话。据说发酵时听到的笑话越好笑，酿成的佳酿就会越带劲。
t_en: The Fools tell jokes to these barrels every day. Rumor says the better the jokes heard during fermentation, the better the wine kicks.

t_ch: 偶尔会有悲悼伶人潜入进来，对着酒桶开始发了狂似地伤春悲秋。这样酿成的醋质量也很高，仙舟人很喜欢，蘸饺子蘸包子都很好吃，拿来做锅包肉更是一绝。
t_en: Sometimes, Mourning Actors sneak in to weep over the barrels. It turns out that much sorrow makes for top-tier vinegar. Xianzhou folks love that stuff for their dumplings and buns, and it's downright divine on sweet-and-sour pork.

t_ch: 标语
t_en: Slogan

t_ch: 告示牌上用不容置疑的口吻写道：「阿哈、悲悼伶人，禁止入内！」
t_en: The sign states in an unequivocal tone: "No Entry for Aha and Mourning Actors!"

t_ch: 酒桶
t_en: Wine barrel

t_ch: 同一个桶里似乎同时存在着红葡萄佳酿、白葡萄佳酿和不含葡萄的葡萄佳酿。不合逻辑？拜托…这可是假面愚者的酒窖！
t_en: Red wine, white wine, and even "grape-less" grape wine all seem to come from the same barrel. Doesn't make sense? Well, this is a Masked Fool's cellar, after all!

t_ch: 你隐约记得，花火把桑博的愚者面具藏在了这的酒桶里。（注：详见《假面双人舞》。）说不定还有哪个酒桶中也藏着点宝贝？
t_en: You vaguely remember that Sparkle hid Sampo's Fool's Mask in one of these wine barrels. (Note: See Masquerade Duet.) Who knows, maybe there are more treasures tucked away here?

t_ch: 笑话与佳酿都能让人沉醉，但它们之间亦有区别：酿好的原浆要进行陈酿，写好的笑话却要趁着新鲜讲出。
t_en: Jokes and alcohol can both intoxicate, but here's the difference: unfiltered beer needs aging, while a good joke needs to be told while it's fresh.

t_ch: 洗手池
t_en: Sink

t_ch: 镜子里映不出你的面庞——因为你太完美了，你的魅力无法被一面无趣的镜子所体现。绝对不是因为我们技术上做不到低场景性能消耗的真镜面反射。绝对不是。
t_en: The mirror can't reflect your face, because you're too perfect, your charm simply can't be captured by a boring old mirror. It's definitely not because we technically can't handle real-time mirror reflections without tanking the performance. Definitely not.

t_ch: 水龙头搭载了「情绪感应系统」，能根据使用者当时的心情流出与之对应的液体，包括但不限于：汽水、清酒、麦茶、辣椒油……
t_en: The faucet is equipped with an "emotion Sensing System" that dispenses liquids corresponding to the user's current mood, including but not limited to: soda, sake, barley tea, chili oil...

t_ch: 愚者们从一位求药使那里买来了这里的洗手液，代价是帮助他对付自己那凶暴的老丈人。这种绿色的洗手液不但能疗伤止血，就连断肢再生也不在话下。
t_en: The Fools bought this hand sanitizer from an Elixir Seeker, in exchange for helping him deal with his ferocious father-in-law. This green hand sanitizer not only heals wounds and stops bleeding, but can even regenerate severed limbs like it's no big deal.

t_ch: 马桶生花
t_en: Blooming potty

t_ch: 出淤泥而不染，濯清涟而不妖。
t_en: Like a lotus rising from the mud, untainted. Bathed in clear ripples, yet natural and pure.

t_ch: 假面愚者有自己的园林艺术。
t_en: The Masked Fools have their own art of gardening.

t_ch: 品味独特的WIFI热点装置，可通过有序按压后方冲水键，进行网络连接。
t_en: A uniquely stylish Wi-Fi hotspot device that connects to the network by pressing the rear flush buttons in the correct sequence.

t_ch: 办公室
t_en: Office

t_ch: 在酒馆里怎么会有个办公室？难道这帮人喝完佳酿以后还要加班？那也太惨了吧！
t_en: Why is there an office in the tavern? Don't tell me these folks have to work overtime after drinking! That's just too sad!

t_ch: 屏幕上正在运行一款帝垣琼玉游戏、一款挂机经营游戏、《银河战力党Online》、以及《折纸小鸟对对碰》…到底什么神人能同时玩这么多游戏？
t_en: The screen's running a Celestial Jade game, an idle management game, Cosmicon Collective Online, and Origami Bird Clash... Who on the cosmos can play this many games at once?

t_ch: 据说有些愚者来到这里，假装进行「严肃」「正常」的工作来作为休闲。看来，任何「欢愉」一旦成为了日常生活，也就和「加班」没啥区别了。
t_en: Word has it that some Fools come here pretending to do "serious" and "normal" work as a form of recreation. Well, it seems once any Elation becomes part of daily life, it's no different from overtime.

t_ch: 折纸小鸟
t_en: Origami Bird

t_ch: 这是什么？折纸小鸟！没想到在匹诺康尼梦境之外的地方，竟然还能见到这些小东西。
t_en: What's this? An Origami Bird! Never thought I'd see these little things outside of Penacony's dreamscape.

t_ch: 酒馆内算是个版权的法外之地，你能看到各种盗版制品，这些折纸小鸟也不例外。最好不要把它们带出酒馆，会给你带来法律上的麻烦。
t_en: The tavern is pretty much a copyright-free zone, where you'll find all sorts of bootleg goods, and these Origami Birds are no exception. Better not take them outside the tavern, though, or you might find yourself in some legal hot water.

t_ch: 为了你的听力健康，不建议与之进行对话：因为假面愚者量产版折纸小鸟的叫声不是「啾啾啾~」，而是「呀啊哈哈哈哈哈——（约<unbreak>110</unbreak>分贝）」
t_en: For the sake of your hearing health, conversation is not recommended: because the mass-produced Masked Fool Origami Birds don't go "tweet tweet tweet~," they go "NYAHAHAHAHAHA——(approx. <unbreak>110</unbreak> decibels)"

t_ch: 画框1
t_en: Frame 1

t_ch: 认识你自己。
t_en: Know Thyself

t_ch: 画框2
t_en: Frame 2

t_ch: 你能肉身接黄泉一刀？焚风都不能肉身接黄泉一刀。你比焚风都牛█。
t_en: You can take Acheron's blade head-on? Even Zephyro can't take Acheron's blade head-on. You're even more bad█ss than Zephyro.

t_ch: 画框3
t_en: Frame 3

t_ch: 生生不息，繁荣昌盛。
t_en: May life be eternal and prosperity flourish.

t_ch: 画框4
t_en: Frame 4

t_ch: 慧极必伤。
t_en: Too much wisdom leads to sorrow.

t_ch: 画框5
t_en: Frame 5

t_ch: 从祂响起的那刻起，你的一天就被毁灭了。
t_en: From the moment THEY rang out, your day was doomed.

t_ch: 画框6
t_en: Frame 6

t_ch: 如果第一幕出现了一把枪，那么第三幕就要击发它。
t_en: If a gun appears in the first act, it must be fired by the third.

t_ch: 画框7
t_en: Frame 7

t_ch: 尚未开垦的未来。
t_en: A future yet untilled.

t_ch: 画框8
t_en: Frame 8

t_ch: 先射箭再画靶子，何尝不是一种百发百中？
t_en: If you shoot first and then draw the bullseye around the arrow, you'll never miss, right?

t_ch: 画框9
t_en: Frame 9

t_ch: 真相是记忆的女儿。
t_en: Truth is the daughter of Memory.

t_ch: 画框<unbreak>10</unbreak>
t_en: Frame <unbreak>10</unbreak>

t_ch: 一副异常精美的画作，细节精细度似乎已经超出了这个宇宙的限度。很多愚者光是凝视这幅画，就陷入了疯狂，口中还念念有词：「█海市█汇区█林街道█林路███号█！」
t_en: An exceptionally exquisite artwork, with a level of detail that seems to transcend the limits of this universe. Many Fools who merely gazed at this painting descended into madness, muttering incessantly: "█Grand Fair█ District█ Forest Street█ Forest Road███ No.█!"

t_ch: 黄金马桶
t_en: Golden Toilet

t_ch: 你靠近这散发无上威光的宝座，毫无疑问，它是纯金的。
t_en: You draw near the throne that is radiating an aura of supreme power. It's unquestionably made of solid gold.

t_ch: 构成它的每一颗原子曾来自中子星的碰撞、超新星的爆发…但假面愚者却将星星们珍贵的泪水锻造成了承接人类烦恼的器皿。也许在他们眼中，这样便幽了宇宙一默。
t_en: Every one of its atoms was born from colliding neutron stars and exploding supernovas... Yet the Masked Fools have forged these precious tears of the stars into a receptacle for humanity's woes. Perhaps, in their eyes, this is their way of telling the universe a joke.

t_ch: 你之前的经历当真只是个废弃的荒诞剧本？但你分明能从眼前的马桶身上，看到那身为帝皇的威仪。
t_en: Was your experience really just some discarded absurd script? Yet you swear you see the majesty of an emperor in this toilet.

t_ch: 高档VIP包间，仅供身份最尊贵的客人使用。
t_en: A premium VIP suite, reserved only for the most distinguished guests.

t_ch: 宝箱
t_en: Chest

t_ch: 你总觉得，刚才打开的那个东西，似乎不是一个通常意义上的「宝箱」。
t_en: You can't shake the feeling that whatever you just opened definitely wasn't a "chest" in the traditional sense.

t_ch: 宝箱！我们是宝箱！我们终于变成宝箱啦！耶！
t_en: Chest! We're chests! We finally turned into treasure chests! Woohoo!

t_ch: 你个傻子，我们明明是宝箱怪！
t_en: You dummy, we're mimics, obviously!

t_ch: 有什么区别吗？
t_en: What's the difference?

t_ch: 没有区别！但是这样比较*怪*！
t_en: No difference! But it's just cooler this way!

t_ch: 电话亭
t_en: Phone Booth

t_ch: 这个电话亭紧锁着门。在门框上不起眼的某处，贴着一块小小的告示「此『时间和空间相对维度』正在维修中，请勿擅自使用。」
t_en: The phone booth's door is locked tight. In an inconspicuous spot on the doorframe, there's a small notice: "This 'Time and Spatial Relative Dimension' is currently under maintenance. Unauthorized use prohibited."

t_ch: 这间电话亭的内部空间远大于外部，据说其中有一半是冰柜。毕竟时空旅行者们经常会去往一些荒芜的位面，这时就要确保充足的炸鱼薯条供应。
t_en: The interior of this phone booth is far larger than its exterior, and rumor has it that half of it is a freezer. After all, time travelers often venture into desolate dimensions, so they need to ensure an adequate supply of fish and chips.

t_ch: VIP包间
t_en: VIP Suite

t_ch: 模因病毒？这么危险的东西为什么会出现在这里…假面愚者怎么什么东西都敢拿来当玩具？
t_en: A memetic virus? Why would something this dangerous be here... Is there anything the Masked Fools wouldn't turn into a plaything?

t_ch: 掀开松垮垮的盖子，只见空桶中央，正端坐着一只被涂花脸的花火娃娃，前方还摆了张画有笑脸的纸条——「诚挚感谢花火小姐帮忙『保管』我的面具，来日必将涌、泉、相、报。」
t_en: Lifting the flimsy lid reveals a Sparkle Doll with a painted face, sitting upright in the center of the empty barrel. In front of it is a note with a smiley face drawn on it: "My sincerest thanks to Miss Sparkle for 'keeping' my mask. I shall one day return the favor... in full."

t_ch: 「带有花火气息的酒桶」
t_en: "A barrel with the scent of Sparkle"

t_ch: 娱乐室
t_en: Game Room

t_ch: 二相乐园的一项社会学研究显示，娱乐室的数量与当地犯罪率呈负相关，你明白了吗？娱乐室是民事调解的中坚力量。
t_en: A sociological study in Planarcadia found that the more game rooms an area has, the lower its crime rate. Do you get it? Game rooms are the core of settling civil issues.

t_ch: 据说店内挂着历史上著名业内大神们的肖像画，也不知道其中有没有青雀。如果没有，那你显然很难认可这个名人堂的含金量。
t_en: They say portraits of legendary players hang inside, but you wonder if Qingque made the cut. If not, you can't really take this hall of fame's lineup seriously.

t_ch: 输了一晚上的人总爱发誓「再也不来了」，但第二天又准时出现，仿佛娱乐室有种魔力，能把昨天的记忆变成今天的筹码。
t_en: A person who loses all night will swear "never again," but they're always back the next day. It's as if the room has a magic that turns the memory of a loss into the stake for the next game.

t_ch: 不死神探事务所
t_en: Ashen Detective Agency

t_ch: 昏暗压抑的灯光、堆积如山的卷宗、错综复杂的线索板、喝了一半的提神饮料——完美符合侦探事务所的刻板印象。
t_en: Dim lighting, mountains of case files, a tangled web of clues on the wall, and half-empty energy drinks everywhere... a perfect match for the stereotypical image of a detective agency.

t_ch: 海报上戴眼镜的中年男人双手十指交叉，眼镜片反射出冷峻的光芒审视着你，下书一行小字：「坏家伙最严厉的父亲。」好像是不死途心目中的自己。
t_en: On the poster, a middle-aged man in glasses clasps his hands, his lenses glinting coldly as he sizes you up. The caption reads: "The sternest father a villain could ask for." This seems to be how Ashveil sees himself.

t_ch: 屏幕没有关，虽然窥屏是不好的习惯，但你还是「不小心」瞥到了一眼，上面是侦探社本季度的营收情况。省流：纯亏损。
t_en: The screen is on. And while snooping is a bad habit, you "can't help" but glance at it. It's the agency's financial report for the quarter. Long story short: It's all red.

t_ch: 桌球店
t_en: Pool Hall

t_ch: 门口贴着这样的标语：「给我一根球杆，我能撬动整个二相乐园」。
t_en: A sign at the entrance reads: "Give me a pool cue, and I can rock the foundations of Planarcadia."

t_ch: 曾经有两位科学家在这里重复进行了五次击球实验，得出了一个物理学上的基本定律：想要赢球，就要把球打进袋口。
t_en: Two scientists are said to have repeatedly conducted five-shot experiments here, leading to a fundamental law of physics: To win the game, one must sink the balls into the pockets.

t_ch: 这是一家无限制桌球店，所谓无限制，就是在传统的桌球游戏中，增加了技能。最近这家店有点转向综合格斗馆的趋势。
t_en: This is a no-holds-barred pool hall. "No-holds-barred" means that on top of the traditional game, you now have abilities. The place is slowly turning into more of an MMA gym.

t_ch: 《苍天航路绒绒号》海报
t_en: Fluffy Across the Blue Poster

t_ch: 眼前的海报虽然精美，但和杰帕德的艺术创作比起来，还是欠缺了一些灵魂。
t_en: This poster is exquisitely designed. But compared to the artistic creations of Gepard, it's missing a certain something.

t_ch: 红粉灰绿棕，绒绒号的主要角色生动形象且各具特色，简直可以直接拿去做什么运动会的吉祥物。列车欢迎你，为你开天辟地。
t_en: Red, pink, grey, green, brown. The Fluffy main cast are so lively and distinct, they could easily be mascots for some sports event. Byline: The Express welcomes you, trailblazing a path to the future.

t_ch: 你的浣熊形象身居C位，你很高兴；你的黑眼圈画得过重了，你不喜欢。
t_en: You're happy to see your raccoon persona taking the center spot. You're less happy about how heavy they went with the dark circles under your eyes.

t_ch: 海报
t_en: Poster

t_ch: 这张海报上的帕姆看起来小小一只，但这只是近大远小的原理。它其实和动物塑的你们差不多高，相当之高大威猛。
t_en: The Pom-Pom on this poster looks tiny, but that's just forced perspective. Pom-Pom is actually as tall as the rest of you in your animal forms: tall and mighty.

t_ch: 海报上的标语写着：「随光开拓未知是方向，继承阿基维利的愿望」。
t_en: The poster's slogan reads: "Follow the light and trailblaze the unknown. Inherit the will of Akivili."

t_ch: 你发现画面中有一处细节不甚合理——列车高速行驶的时候严禁打开远光灯，否则会晃瞎对向飞船驾驶者的眼睛。
t_en: You spot a minor inaccuracy in the image: It's strictly forbidden to turn on the high beams while the Express is in motion, as it would blind the pilots of any oncoming vessels.

t_ch: 零食角
t_en: Snack Pantry

t_ch: 狸狸通信杂志社有着全行业首屈一指的福利待遇，比如这个无限供应饮料和小吃的零食角——想必有一名财大气粗的社长在撑腰吧。
t_en: Furbobocom Magazine offers some of the best perks in the business, like this snack corner with its endless supply of drinks and treats... They must have a pretty loaded president behind their back.

t_ch: 出于员工身体健康的考虑，狸狸通信杂志社曾经短暂地将所有饮料小吃都换成了低愿力浓度版本，但最终因零食角变得无人问津而作罢。
t_en: Out of concern for employees' health, Furbobocom Magazine once briefly switched everything to low-Wishpower versions. They had to abandon the plan when no one went to the snack corner anymore.

t_ch: 虽然马赛克级别的贴图精度让这些食物看起来没有那么美味，但是请相信我，它们真的很好吃。
t_en: The pixelated, low-res textures might not make the food look very appetizing, but trust me, it's actually delicious.

t_ch: 盒子里的伊磨吉
t_en: Emoti in a Box

t_ch: 这些伊磨吉一幅幅可怜巴巴的样子，似乎是遭到遗弃了。可惜你没条件收养伊磨吉，只能祈祷它们能遇到愿意收养的好心人。
t_en: These poor emotis look so miserable, as if they've been abandoned. Unfortunately, you're in no position to adopt one. You can only hope they find a kind soul to take them home.

t_ch: 从纸盒的磨损程度看，这群伊磨吉已经在在此处生活很久了。也不知道二相乐园有没有野生伊磨吉收容中心。
t_en: From the state of the cardboard box, this group of emotis has been here for a very long time. You wonder if there's a wild emoti rescue center anywhere in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 面对困境，伊磨吉表现出不同的人生态度，有人抓狂、有人崩溃，也有人微笑面对。
t_en: Faced with their predicament, the emotis are showing different attitudes. Some are freaking out, some are having a complete breakdown, but a few are managing to smile through the pain.

t_ch: 涂鸦
t_en: Graffiti

t_ch: 也许这幅涂鸦表达的含义是：「当画笔接触干枯的手指，后者将变得鲜活起来。」啊…原来是美甲店的广告，那没事了。
t_en: Maybe this graffiti means, "When a brush touches a withered hand, it springs back to life." Oh... it's just an ad for a nail salon. Never mind.

t_ch: 当心，别靠太近，楼上有两个伊磨吉。「被滚落的伊磨吉砸中」是二相乐园相当常见的受伤原因，这些小家伙还挺重的，
t_en: Watch out, don't get too close. There are two emotis up there. "Struck by a falling emoti" is a surprisingly common cause of injury in Planarcadia. Those little guys are heavier than they look.

t_ch: 二相乐园对街头艺术极其包容，法律允许创作者在任何公共区域创作涂鸦。但是画得太烂是违法行为，最高可判处「强制绘艺补习」。
t_en: Planarcadia is exceptionally tolerant of street art. The law allows artists to create graffiti in any communal area. However, creating terrible art is a punishable offense, with a maximum sentence of "mandatory art tutoring."

t_ch: 涂鸦
t_en: Graffiti

t_ch: 你所看到的是街头涂鸦作品《以我残躯化作炽火》。
t_en: The piece of street art before you is titled Don't Fear the Reaper.

t_ch: 画中人是一位摇滚巨星「强尼猫手」，他的歌曲以叛逆精神鼓舞着一代人，最终因试图爆破珠星财团而被捕入狱。
t_en: The man in the painting is rock legend "Johnny Cathand," whose songs of rebellious spirit inspired an entire generation. He was eventually imprisoned for attempting to blow up the Pearluxe Corp HQ.

t_ch: 作者在这幅作品中注入了不少愿力，所以当你把耳朵贴在涂鸦上的时候，还能听到画中乐手低哑的嘶吼。
t_en: The artist poured a significant amount of Wishpower into this work. So if you put your ear to the wall, you can still hear the musician's raw, raspy howls.

t_ch: 帝垣琼玉
t_en: Celestial Jade

t_ch: 其实伊磨吉并不适合玩棋牌类游戏，因为从他们的脸上就能直接看出手中牌的好坏。
t_en: Emotis aren't really suited for tile games, because you can tell whether their hand is good or bad just by looking at their faces.

t_ch: 法律规定，只有球体直径大于半米的伊磨吉可以进入娱乐室。但如果直径大于一米，那往往又会被禁止入内，因为会撞到其他顾客。
t_en: The law stipulates that only emoti with a diameter of over half a meter may enter a game room. But if their diameter exceeds one meter, they're usually barred from entry because they tend to bump into other customers.

t_ch: 伊磨吉看起来憨态可掬，但滚起来风驰电掣。曾有打输牌的伊磨吉为了赖账，与治安官们进行了一场紧张刺激的公路追逐，骨碌骨碌骨碌。
t_en: Emotis may look harmless, but when they get rolling, they're lightning-fast. Once, an emoti tried to dash after losing a game, leading the officers on a high-speed chase. Roll, roll, roll...

t_ch: 办公室落地窗
t_en: Office Floor-To-Ceiling Window

t_ch: 怪不得大佬们都喜欢把办公室设在大楼顶层，站在这里往下眺望，确实有种睥睨天下、掌控世界的感觉。
t_en: No wonder the bigwigs love having their offices on the top floor. Standing here looking down, you really do get the feeling that you're the king of the world.

t_ch: 远处万家灯火，天上点点星光，而你孤独的站在诺大的豪华办公室，有那么点高处不胜寒。
t_en: The city lights twinkle in the distance, mirroring the stars above. And you stand alone in this massive, luxurious office. It feels a little lonely at the top.

t_ch: 眺望幻月
t_en: Gazing at the Phantasmoon

t_ch: 据说月亮的运行规律深刻影响着人类的情绪，幻月离二相乐园如此之近，也难怪这里的人们情绪充沛、思如泉涌。
t_en: The lunar cycle is said to have a profound effect on human emotion. With the Phantasmoon this close to Planarcadia, it's no wonder the people here are so full of feeling and brimming with inspiration.

t_ch: 不知哪里飘荡来的音乐：「Fly me to the stars，And let me play among the  light」。
t_en: Music drifts in from out of nowhere: "Fly me to the star, and let me play among the light."

t_ch: 在某个遥远的星系，那里的人类试图用行星发动机推走他们的「月球」，不过因为一点小意外，「月球」最后被炸成了碎片。
t_en: In a distant planetary system, its inhabitants once tried to propel their "moon" away with planetary engines. But due to a minor accident, it ended up being blown to bits.

t_ch: 车祸现场
t_en: Scene of a Car Crash

t_ch: 伊磨吉的外壳由愿力组成，但内部却是空的。这座城市中的大部分生物都差不多，外表坚硬，内心却只有空洞。
t_en: An emoti's shell is formed from Wishpower, but it's empty inside. It's the same for most creatures in this city: a hard exterior with nothing but a hollow heart.

t_ch: 辅助驾驶技术的普及让伊磨吉也有了驾驶汽车的资格，不过紧急情况下它们可没有脚踩刹车。
t_en: With assisted driving becoming widespread, emotis now qualify to drive cars as well. Problem is, they can't hit the brakes with their feet in emergencies.

t_ch: 伊磨吉过马路的时候一定要飘得高一些，这样做可以大幅降低被车撞的风险。
t_en: When crossing the road, emotis should float a bit higher. Doing so greatly reduces the risk of getting hit by cars.

t_ch: 共愿帮装饰
t_en: Synwish Syndicate Decorations

t_ch: 一般上了年纪的人总喜欢培养点烧钱的爱好，比如钓鱼和摄影。共愿帮的大佬们最热衷于文玩收藏，似乎因为这样比较容易让人看出来。
t_en: Older folks usually pick up pricey hobbies like fishing or photography. The big bosses of the Synwish Syndicate, though? They're all about collecting flashy fandangles, mostly so everyone can see how rich they are.

t_ch: 一幅噗噜贵族的画作吸引了你的目光，水平相当之高，是共愿帮成员哈耶克的杰作。此人亦是「乐园三大肖像画家」之一，但因为没人见过它的脸而鲜少提及。
t_en: A painting of a ploo noble caught your eye. It's actually a masterpiece by Hayek, a Synwish Syndicate member. He's one of the arcadia's top three portrait artists, though people rarely talk about him since nobody knows what he looks like.

t_ch: 冰柜
t_en: Freezer

t_ch: 这是冰柜，不是时间停止柜，食物放久了也会坏，希望每个人都能养成定期清理的习惯。
t_en: This is a freezer, not a time-stopping cabinet. Food will still go bad if it sits here for too long, so I hope everyone can develop a habit of cleaning it out regularly.

t_ch: 据说某些餐厅只招两种员工，冰柜管理员和微波炉使用师，如果你两者都会，那你就是首席大厨了。
t_en: They say some restaurants only hire two types of staff members: freezer managers and microwave operators. If you can do both, then congratulations, you're the head chef.

t_ch: 在没有发明冰柜之前，食物放不了太久，所以餐厅必须现炒现做。
t_en: Before freezers were invented, food couldn't be stored for long, so restaurants had to cook everything fresh to order.

t_ch: 害怕被打进洞的伊磨吉
t_en: Emoti Afraid of Being Punched Into a Pocket

t_ch: 伊磨吉的成长过程充满了艰辛，在它们直径增长的过程中，可能会不断地充当成乒乓球、桌球、高尔夫球、棒球、篮球……
t_en: The growth of an emoti is full of hardships. As their diameter increases, they often end up serving as ping-pong balls, billiard balls, golf balls, baseballs, basketballs...

t_ch: 虽然每个伊磨吉看起来都差不多圆，但总有一些比另一些更圆。
t_en: Although every emoti looks more or less round, some are definitely rounder than others.

t_ch: 桌球老手都喜欢挑选比较年轻的伊磨吉做击发母球，因为这样的伊磨吉还心怀梦想，情绪波动大，爆发力更强。
t_en: Pool sharks prefer picking younger emoti as their cue ball, since younger ones still have dreams, stronger emotional swings, and pack a stronger punch.

t_ch: 猫咖广告
t_en: Cat Cafe Ad

t_ch: 标语上写着：「现磨咖啡，极致醇香，一口上头。」
t_en: The sign reads: "Freshly ground coffee, rich and irresistible, one sip gets you going."

t_ch: 一家以「航天员」为主题的咖啡店，店员会穿着航天服，在模拟无重力的特殊包厢用软铝管喂你咖啡。
t_en: An astronaut-themed coffee shop where the staff wears spacesuits and feeds you coffee through soft aluminum tubes in special zero-gravity simulation booths.

t_ch: 广告上的人物是咖啡店的招牌特调师「超级梦幻」，无人知道她的真实姓名，只因她调制的咖啡有梦幻般的香气而得名。
t_en: The person in the advertisement is the café's star barista, "Super Dreamy." No one knows her real name. She earned this nickname because the coffee she brews has a magical, dreamy aroma.

t_ch: 食物招牌
t_en: Food Sign

t_ch: 许多小吃店都以「阿哈品尝后开怀大笑」为卖点。一位星神的胃口这么好，不禁让人怀疑来的到底是欢愉还是贪饕。
t_en: Many snack shops boast that "Aha bursts into laughter after tasting." With an Aeon having such a big appetite, one can't help but wonder if it's Elation or Voracity that stopped by.

t_ch: 曾经锅气缭绕的平价美食街，自从公司引入「高效餐饮供应链管理」的理念后，开始出现越来越多的预制菜。
t_en: The once-bustling food street filled with the aroma of sizzling woks has seen more and more pre-made meals ever since the IPC introduced their concept of "efficient food supply chain management."

t_ch: 这条街每隔不久就会出现一家网红店，今天也许是「狮子头哥」，明天也可能「鲷鱼烧姐」，说不上多么好吃，但就是有很多人排队。
t_en: New trendy shops pop up on this street every other week. It's "Meatball Man" one day and "Sweetfin Waffle Lady" the next. The food isn't even that great, but people just love to wait in line.

t_ch: 打针的伊磨吉
t_en: Needle-Wielding Emoti

t_ch: 据说这家诊所专门服务不敢或不能去正规医院接受治疗的病人。不过即使是罪大恶极之人，也应该先治好病再接受审判。这样想来，这家诊所倒也算在做好事。
t_en: It's said that this clinic specializes in treating patients who are too scared or unable to go to regular hospitals. But even the worst criminals deserve medical care before facing judgment. In that sense, this clinic is doing some good after all.

t_ch: 这家诊所的医生会选择一些看起来很恐怖但莫名有效的激进疗法，实在有点「但管人直，不管人死」的意思。
t_en: The doctors at this clinic tend to use some terrifying yet mysteriously effective radical treatments. Their approach is basically: "As long as it fixes your problem, who cares if you die?"

t_ch: 招牌上的广告词：「<unbreak>24</unbreak>小时营业，除了感情不治，其他什么都治」。
t_en: Their sign reads: "Open <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7. We cure everything except broken hearts."

t_ch: 恋爱的伊磨吉
t_en: Love-Struck Emoti

t_ch: 有那么多地方可以约会，电影院、咖啡厅、水族馆、游乐场，这对伊磨吉却选择了最浪漫的水泥管。
t_en: With so many places available for a date, such as the cinema, a café, the aquarium, and an amusement park, this pair of emotis chose the most romantic location: the concrete pipe.

t_ch: 这两个家伙干脆原地结婚算了，要不让后面那只伊磨吉当你们的证婚人吧。
t_en: These two might as well get married right here and now. Hey, why not have that emoti behind them be their wedding officiant?

t_ch: 两只沉浸在爱河里的伊磨吉，完全没有注意到另一头还蹲着一位伤心人。
t_en: The two love-struck emotis were so lost in their own little world that they completely failed to notice another heartbroken soul crouching on the other side.
